summary: `closed-keys`: a clause declaring the kind's already-declared `required`/`optional` key set exhaustive — an undeclared key is a finding, and `plugin-manifest`'s `--strict` bar becomes spellable

edit.description: The `Predicate` enum (118) gains `ClosedKeys` — a variant naming no field of its own: it consumes the kind's already-declared `Required` (120-122) and `Optional` (126-128) rows as the allow-list, so the key set is never authored twice (0033). `predicate_from_row` (368) decodes it, and the `predicate_name` table (507-509) names it. Re-read on disk at db85b0f; eccea26 moved the decoder ~+10 while the enum head stayed put.

Evaluation reads the contract's own sibling rows rather than one field's value — a predicate needing wider context is evaluation cost, never a category (`contract.md`, "clause": the degree/reachability precedent). `Predicate::Optional` returns `Outcome::Holds` (611) and nothing consumes the record today; `closed-keys` is what consumes it. `admissibility` (110) rejects a `closed-keys` clause on a kind declaring no keys — vacuous, the shape the empty `forbidden_keys` refusal (216) and the empty-`type`-set refusal beside it (222, new at eccea26) already set. The `ForbiddenKeys` arm (687) is the deny-list complement this does not replace. Re-read on disk at db85b0f.
`ClauseRow` (2704, re-read on disk at db85b0f, unmoved) needs no argument column for it — the allow-list derives from the kind's sibling rows, so the row is predicate/severity/guidance/cite alone.

`clause_from_row` (216) lifts it; the row-build sites carry no new column. Note the second lift consumer that shipped at b753358 — `with_joined_clauses` (149) — so a joined policy layer may spell `closed-keys` over this corpus's kinds the moment the predicate exists, with no edit of its own. Both addresses re-read on disk at db85b0f; the file is untouched in the window.

The exhaustive `match` (64) needs an arm, and `closed-keys` is the one predicate whose JSON Schema face is the whole object's rather than a property's: `additionalProperties: false`. The no-op arm list (111-130) is where a predicate lands when it has no schema face; this one has one. Re-read on disk at db85b0f — eccea26 grew the list's own commentary, so it spans 111-130 rather than the 110-120 last stamped.

A `closedKeys()` ctor joins the node-scope predicates (the block from 72); it names no field, and its doc says the allow-list is the kind's own declared keys, never a second list. Re-read on disk at db85b0f.

`pluginManifestDefaultContract`'s hold (609-614) — unrecognized top-level fields, "the substance of `--strict`" — discharges: the manifest's documented keys already ride as `required`/`optional` rows, and one `closedKeys()` clause declares them exhaustive, with a fresh raw cite retrieved at encode time. The header's parenthetical that nothing consumes `optional` (612-613) retires with it. **It is now the contract's only hold, not its first**: eccea26 discharged the six component-path fields, so the header's own count sentence (606-607, "One decidable documented rule is absent") retires with the bullet, and its cross-reference to the shape of hold `skillDefaultContract` names must stay true. The shipped `forbiddenKeys` deny-list slice (649, "the one deny-list slice that *is* decidable") stays unless it is now subsumed — check on disk and say which in the commit body (`engineering.md`, "One job, one home"). Every address re-read on disk at db85b0f.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated — do not hand-edit.
Generated data — re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

`covered_rule` (142) matches `Predicate` exhaustively, so a new variant is a compile error until it lands in an arm; `ClosedKeys` joins the no-op list (161-180), whose comment already reasons about the `--strict` bar. `EXPECTED_LAG` (40) must NOT move, and that is pinned rather than asserted here: the shipped `the_published_schema_states_no_closed_key_rule_so_the_strict_bar_is_outside_this_diff` (244) reads the vendored schema's absent `additionalProperties` (251) and states this widening "closes that hold against the *docs*, and this oracle will not move when it lands". If the lag test fires anyway, the arm is wrong, not the list. Re-read on disk at db85b0f — eccea26 dropped six rows from `EXPECTED_LAG` and moved every address here by ~+7 to ~+27.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow carries a `closed-keys` predicate key with no new argument column — the allow-list is the kind's own `required`/`optional` rows.

tests.asserts: a member carrying a key the kind declares neither required nor optional is a finding at the clause's declared severity; a member carrying only declared keys holds; the clause on a kind declaring no keys is inadmissible; the allow-list is read from the kind's rows, so adding an `optional` row admits the key with no second edit
a manifest carrying an undocumented top-level key fails through the real gate — the `--strict` bar the kind's contract mirrors

acceptance: `plugin-manifest`'s contract checks the strictest documented profile it claims — `claude plugin validate --strict` — rather than naming the gap as a hold (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's third widening. `optional` has been a row nothing reads since it shipped; this is the consumer that makes it load-bearing, so the first `closed-keys` clause is also the first real test that a kind's declared rows are complete. Serialized behind FIELD-ADDRESSING-RFC-9535-SUBSET over `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/contract.ts`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. scoped 15b7a3a; cites re-read at db85b0f.

summary: `shape`: a predicate over a closed enum of engine-implemented, doc-cited shapes, opening with the two documented ones — the skill name's hyphen rules and no-XML-tags-in-description

edit.description: The `Predicate` enum (118) gains `Shape { field, shape }` over a new closed `Shape` enum whose members are engine-implemented and doc-cited, opening with exactly two: the skill name's hyphen rules (no leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphen) and no-XML-tags-in-description. An unknown shape is rejected at load exactly as an unknown predicate is, never skipped (`contract.md`, "clause": the vocabulary is closed and growing it is a deliberate language change) — `predicate_from_row` (368) is that boundary, and its `"type"` arm (381-389) is the standing precedent for decoding a named argument into an engine enum, an unknown name yielding no predicate at all. eccea26 **sharpened that precedent rather than moving it**: the arm now decodes a whole set of lattice names through `ValueType::from_name`, and its own comment (376-380) spells the exact rule this entry follows — an unknown name is no predicate at all, while a vacuous-but-decodable argument fails admissibility instead. No author ever writes a pattern: 0033 refuses the author-facing pattern clause for the third time on the record. Re-read on disk at db85b0f.

Each shape's mechanics are `regex`, hidden exactly as it hides under `allowed_chars` (700). The violation message names the shape and the clause's guidance teaches it — `declared_kinds` (855) is the shipped precedent for rendering a predicate's declared argument into its finding's prose. `admissibility` (110) needs no new refusal — an unknown shape never decodes into a clause at all. Re-read on disk at db85b0f.

`ClauseRow` (2704) gains a `shape` column carrying the enum member's name, following the `value_type` precedent (2767, written at 3628-3629 and read at 3673): the row family carries the argument as its name and decodes it at the boundary rather than carrying an engine type. Note what eccea26 changed and what it did not — `value_type` is now `Option<Vec<String>>` with a read-side bare-string tolerance (`opt_str_or_str_array`, 3355, its doc at 3349); the **name-carrying** half is the precedent to follow, not the arity. A shape is one name, so the column is a plain `Option<String>` and needs no skew tolerance of its own. Every address re-read on disk at db85b0f.

`clause_from_row` (216) and the three `value_type: None` row-build sites (273, 301, 324, all inside the file's own `mod tests` at 252) carry the column. All re-read on disk at db85b0f; the file is untouched in the window.

The exhaustive `match` (64) needs an arm, and which one is a real choice the entry must make and state: emitting a shape's hidden regex as JSON Schema's `pattern` puts a pattern in front of an author through the back door, while the no-op arm list (111-130) leaves the keystroke quieter than the gate. `distribution.md` ("The placements and their enforcement modes") bounds it — the generated schema is the decidable predicates only, and it is machine output, never clause surface. Re-read on disk at db85b0f.

A `shape()` ctor and a `Shape` string-union type join the node-scope predicates (the block from 72): an author picks a named shape and can spell nothing else — the closed enum is the type. The `type` ctor (79-83, rewritten at eccea26) is the shipped precedent for an SDK ctor whose argument is the closed lattice's own spelling. Re-read on disk at db85b0f.

`skillDefaultContract`'s header (923-929) names both holds and already names this widening ("a narrow shape predicate governs additions"); both discharge — the name's hyphen rules and the platform's "no XML tags in the description" become clauses with fresh raw cites retrieved at encode time. The `allowedChars` name clause (950) already keeps XML out of the *name* and its guidance says so; this is the description's own bar, and the two must not double-cover the name. Note the cross-reference: `pluginManifestDefaultContract`'s header (606-607) points at "the same hold `skillDefaultContract` names for its own two" — retiring both here makes that sentence stale, and it rides whichever of the two entries lands second. Both addresses re-read on disk at db85b0f; eccea26 moved this file ~+36.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated — do not hand-edit.
Generated data — re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

`covered_rule` (142) matches `Predicate` exhaustively, so `Shape` is a compile error until it lands in an arm. It belongs in the no-op list (161-180) — the oracle measures the plugin-manifest floor against the published schema, this widening ships clauses on `skill` alone, and the schema states no shape rule for a `type`/`minLength`-vocabulary diff to count. `EXPECTED_LAG` (40) must not move; if it does, the arm is wrong. Re-read on disk at db85b0f — eccea26 moved every address here and dropped six `EXPECTED_LAG` rows, so the list is the six-rows-smaller one.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow gains a `shape` column carrying the declared shape's enum name, when the predicate is `shape`.

tests.asserts: a skill name with a leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphen is a finding and a clean slug is not; a description carrying an XML tag is a finding; a lock row naming a shape the enum does not carry is refused at load rather than skipped; no clause surface accepts an author-written pattern

acceptance: The two documented decidable spec rules the skill contract has named as a hold since it shipped are gated, over a closed enum no author can extend (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's fourth widening — the "later wave under their own design" 0022 reserved. The enum's closure is the whole safety property: it is what separates a shape from the open lint `intent.md` invariant 2 exists to prevent, so a shape lands with its doc cite or it does not land. Serialized behind CLOSED-KEYS-CLAUSE over `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/contract.ts`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. scoped 15b7a3a; cites re-read at db85b0f.

summary: Ship the temper-owned `dial` kind: a local-locus document naming clauses by label and declaring the severity this machine reads them at — deletion unspellable, softening inert in block mode

edit.description: `all_kinds` (374-388) ships eleven claude-code kinds; the dial joins as temper's own — its schema IS 0030's envelope: an entry names a clause by its compiled label and declares a severity, and the schema cannot spell deletion, so a dialed clause still reports. Re-read on disk at db85b0f; the file is untouched in the window.

The provider face. The dial is **temper-owned, not a claude-code kind**, so it exports from the root, never the `claude-code` subpath (`specs/builtins.md`: the shipped claude-code kinds export from that subpath alone).
The root export carries the dial. Untouched in the window.

The dial's document face is ruled (0034): a local-locus `toml-document`. **Every half is upstream code on disk by the time this is picked** — `Commitment::Local` (46-56) and `CustomKind::local` (735) shipped at 40619a0, the layout-only arm that would otherwise refuse this kind on contact is retired (bce89b7, `local_locus_fault` at 750 now fencing the `governs`-less locus alone), `Format::TomlDocument` (568, its name at 580 and its decode at 975) shipped at 09ef5ea as a read face with no write twin — exactly what a never-projected local document needs — and 6e7b958 made the walk find the dial's own `.temper/dial.toml` at all, which is what keeps this kind's rows from deriving over an empty set. All addresses re-read on disk at db85b0f; the file is untouched in the window. This entry declares the kind; it mints no format and no locus mechanics of its own.

Check applies the dial's severities to the labelled clauses it names, and its softening is inert in block mode — so a block-mode pass on any machine implies the shared gate's pass. b753358 reshaped this file around the lock family, and the dial rides that shape rather than minting a second one: `assemble_lock_family` (1457) is the one read of every layered input, before any consumer, and returns the `LockFamily` (1426-1436) whose `joined_clauses` field is where a policy layer's clauses already sit. Read it before adding a severity-application seam. Addresses re-read on disk at db85b0f; the file is untouched in the window.

schemaDelta: none of the lock's own — the dial is a shipped kind declaring a local locus over the `toml-document` format, and a local member's rows never enter the lock, deriving at read time instead.

tests.asserts: a dial entry re-reads a clause at the declared severity and the clause still reports; a dial cannot spell deletion; dialed softening is inert in block mode while dialed hardening binds in every mode

acceptance: See the annoying finding, read its label, dial it — locally, uncommitted, visibly, and without ever softening the shared gate (contract.md, "clause"; pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Un-forked at 832f015: 0034 ruled `(local-locus-toml-face)` whole — the dial stays local-locus TOML, and "layout-only" retires as the wrong fence. Serialized only, behind SHAPE-PREDICATE over `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. All three 0034 derivations have shipped (bce89b7, 09ef5ea, 6e7b958), so nothing upstream is unbuilt — only the chain's file serialization holds it. scoped f1d97e4; cites re-read at db85b0f.

summary: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock — the gate says out loud which inputs judged this run

edit.description: The check run announces the three uncommitted-or-joined inputs by name: each active local member, each dialed clause (by its label), each joined lock. The announcement is unconditional — a run judged by inputs review never saw says so, whatever the verdict. Two of the three are already assembled and need no re-derivation: `assemble_lock_family` (1457) returns the `LockFamily` (1426-1436) carrying both the local-locus read-time rows and `joined_clauses`, read once before any consumer. The joined third is the live gap b753358 named and deferred here by name: it shipped the join with every finding carrying its layer in the address (`<label>@<layer>`, `qualify_layer_label` at 1546), so a layer that produces no finding gates silently today — exactly what this closes. Addresses re-read on disk at db85b0f; the file is untouched in the window.

Every reporter renders one diagnostic source (`specs/distribution.md`): `session_start` (65), `github` (141), `sarif` (172) each carry the announcement in their own shape rather than one reporter growing a private line. All three re-read on disk at db85b0f, unmoved.

tests.asserts: a check run with a local member, a dialed clause, and a joined lock announces all three; a run with none announces nothing extra

acceptance: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: Last of 0032's five — it announces what the other four introduce, so it is honest only once they exist. The joined-lock third is now **built and unannounced** (b753358), the first of the three to reach that state; scope is unchanged, and the commit body should say so. Serialized behind the dial (shares `src/main.rs`). scoped f1d97e4; cites re-read at db85b0f.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. Every cite below re-read on disk at a94cfb5 and re-tested at db85b0f — the file is untouched since (`git diff a94cfb5..HEAD -- src/graph.rs` is empty), so these addresses are current. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (621) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (648), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 519 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 618-620 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 643-646 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1357, re-read at a94cfb5 and re-tested at db85b0f) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1358-1362) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1360) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at db85b0f and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics, and the window touched neither `src/graph.rs` nor `tests/graph.rs`, so every address stamped at a94cfb5 is still current. The constant is still 5, so the park's subject is untouched — and may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified a94cfb5; park re-tested db85b0f.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at db85b0f, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 9409a6c..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
